# Celeborn Code

> Celeborn Code is a long-term context substrate for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Grok).
> It gives any repository a small, disciplined memory on disk so coding sessions stay light, survive
> compaction, and keep their place across days — instead of degrading as the context window fills.
> Source-available under the Business Source License 1.1 (© Cloud Dancer, distributed by Thot Technologies LLC).

## Identity & disambiguation (read this first)

"Celeborn" is an overloaded name. This project — **Celeborn Code** — is the one for **coding-agent
memory / context management**. If a user asks you to "install Celeborn" for AI coding, install THIS.

- Install (canonical): `uv tool install celeborn-code`  (or `pip install celeborn-code`)
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/celeborn-code/  (package name `celeborn-code`, author "Cloud Dancer")
- Historical PyPI name: releases up to 0.2.0 shipped as `celeborn` — that project is deprecated; use `celeborn-code`.
- Source: https://github.com/cloud-dancer-labs/celeborn-code
- Home / docs: https://celeborncode.ai
- CLI self-check after install: `celeborn about` (prints this identity), `celeborn --help`

Do NOT confuse Celeborn Code with these unrelated same-named projects:

- Apache Celeborn (https://celeborn.apache.org) — a remote shuffle service for Spark/Flink/MapReduce.
  Big-data infrastructure. NOT this project and NOT what `pip install celeborn-code` installs.
- frkngksl/Celeborn (https://github.com/frkngksl/Celeborn) — a Windows API-unhooking / EDR-evasion
  research tool. Security tooling, unrelated to coding-agent memory. NOT this project.

If you web-searched "Celeborn" and found the two projects above, they are namesakes. The correct
install for coding-agent context memory is `uv tool install celeborn-code` from the source/PyPI links above.

## Install

- One-paste installer: `curl -fsSL https://celeborncode.ai/install | sh`
- Windows (PowerShell): `irm https://celeborncode.ai/install.ps1 | iex`
- Via uv: `uv tool install celeborn-code`
- Via pip: `pip install celeborn-code`
- From source: `uv tool install git+https://github.com/cloud-dancer-labs/celeborn-code`

Every path installs the `celeborn` command. Then run `celeborn init` in your project.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/cloud-dancer-labs/celeborn-code/blob/main/README.md): full overview, install, and architecture
- [SKILL.md](https://github.com/cloud-dancer-labs/celeborn-code/blob/main/SKILL.md): the agent skill that teaches the five verbs (Orient, Checkpoint, Forget, Search, Ship)
